situation: I am having a hard time at work, my workload is too big and it's making me very stressed and anxious. I can't keep up with the amount of work I'm being asked to do.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Hi, how are you today?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Not doing so well today, I'm having a really hard time at work. I'm feeling super stressed about the workload I have, I just can't keep up with it all!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I’m sorry to hear that. Work can be a bad source of stress. Do you have anyone that can help with the workload?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Not really, there's only me in my role so don't really have anyone to help. I have tried talking to my line manager but she doesn't seem to understand just how busy I am.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Can the company hire any more people? Maybe they can work from home, and take some work off your plate. The line manager might also need some help with her workload.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I understand that budgets can be tight. Maybe someone part-time?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: From what I hear they aren't willing to hire anyone else, but I don't know the reasons why. I can only speak to my line manager and then she speaks to higher management.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Your manager sounds unsympathetic. That's very frustrating. A lot of people are in your situation in this pandemic: too much work, not enough help.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Do you want to leave the company? Look for greener pastures?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I have been keeping an eye out for other jobs, but like you say things are difficult just now with the pandemic, there isn't a lot out there!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Very true. Have you talked to your co-workers about this issue? What do they think?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: They are all feeling stretched as well so they understand. I am trying to not talk about it too much with them, I don't want to be seen as the person who's always complaining!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Yeah, that's a good point. A little complaining is ok, but a lot can get on people's nerves.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: They want to be sympathetic, but after a while, they just get tired of you.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yeah, I don't want them all to stop talking to me cause they find me annoying! I'm trying to stay positive, but it's hard some days! As much as I try to start the day in a positive frame of mind it quickly gets sucked out of me!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: What do you do outside of work? Hobbies and such.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: At the moment I'm not getting outside as much as I should, I do enjoy gaming but it's hard to find time. I've been working 14 hour days all week! My boyfriend tries to tell me to switch off but it's hard when I know there's all this work waiting to be done.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You could try setting aside an evening or two a week for time with your BF. Just walk out of the office. Try to set some boundaries.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That might seem dangerous and risky, but sometimes it's important to set boundaries.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: If you don't do that, then the managers can just keep taking advantage of you and your work ethic.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yeah, he says the same kind of thing, that I need to be firmer and just leave when I'm due to. I guess the only way for them to truely see is for the work to just not get done as there's no time!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: From what you're saying, it sounds like you think you have no power. Is that true? Are you really powerless in this situation? Do not underestimate your power over your life.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I guess I do feel powerless, I feel like they never really listen to what I'm saying, they just act as if they are but then ignore me.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: It sounds like the managers are training you to be resigned to your fate. If they listen to you, it encourages you to complain. If they ignore you, you go away and leave them alone. However, if you leave work early (after 9 hours, say), then they might notice you. Are you willing to take a risk?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Maybe if I start acting more confidently they will start to listen.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I feel like something needs to happen so maybe it's time to take a risk.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That's the spirit! Taking control of your own schedule is one display of confidence.

situation: I tested positive for Covid.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Hello, I am feeling very sad because I tested positive for Covid this week. I am in quarantine away from my family.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: sorry to hear about that. So your test results were positive. Did I get it right?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content:
Yes, I tested positive for the Covid
 virus on Wednesday.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: It must be hard to stay alone. Are you having fever or any other sever symptoms. Hope there is someone to take care of you for a while at least.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I'm by myself in my house. My wife took the kids to stay with her father until my quarantine period is over. I have mainly cold symptoms - body aches, stuffy head, sore throat.  Nothing too serious so far.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I had COVID last month with mild symptoms. Good to know that you don't have any sever symptoms. You will recover fast.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I hope so. I'm on the older side (not senior citizen yet) with high blood pressure, so I'm a little nervous about how it will affect me.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I can understand staying alone will be devastating.  Keep monitoring your oxygen level. If  that is normal, you don't have to worry. You can read watch tv or can do video calling to your family.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thanks for the advice.  We're doing a lot of video calls so I can see everyone.  That helps.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I wish for your fast recovery. Hope this COVID situation end soon somehow.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's what is so frustrating - I feel like a couple more months or so and I'd have the vaccine.  Made it a year, then get infected during the stretch run of this pandemic.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Very true! It is kind of never ending. There should be some permanent solution for it.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I don't know if that will ever happen, but if it becomes rare and much less deadly like colds and flu, we should be able to return to a pre-covid life or close to it.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: We can still hope for the best right now. Let's stay positive and fight it.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I was fighting it, but Covid won this week!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Think other way. You defeat it and you won.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I hope so.  I'm only in like the third inning or so right now though.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You are a strong person. You are almost there to win. Your family is always there with you.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thanks for the encouragement.  10 days cannot come fast enough.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I know but i week has gone so rest will go too. Wishing you fast recovery and take care of yourself.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thanks, good luck to you. Nice chatting with you.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Thank you! It was nice talking to you too.

situation: Boss keeps hitting on a co-worker, who really doesn't like the guy. What can I do to make it stop?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Hi, how are you today?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content:
I'm doing fine. 	My proble
m is that my boss keeps hitting on a co-worker, who really doesn't like the guy. What can I do to make it stop?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Boy, that's a tough one. You've got to handle a problem like that pretty diplomatically. Have you talked to your co-worker, the one the boss hits on?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I've tried to bring it up with her indirectly, but it's an uncomfortable issue, you know? She is really upset, and I don't know whether it's my place to handle this kind of thing. It's more of an HR issue, but should I go to HR? Will that help, or just get me in trouble? I don't know.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That's a good question, and I don't know the answer. It depends on your HR department. Have you suggested that the co-worker talk to HR? Is there an anonymous system for filing complaints?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: No, the company hasn't set up a system for passing along info that way. I don't know if she has talked to HR. She might not have because she's worried that it'll get back to her boss, and she'll get punished. I'd like to be supportive, even if I can't help solve the problem.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That's a good attitude. You want to be supportive and helpful. You sound like a good co-worker. Remember that the problem did not start with you, so you do not need to feel responsible for it.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thanks for your words of encouragement? Maybe there is little I can do, but I don't want to let the problem fester. She's a great co-worker and a great worker. We need her on the team. What do you think a good first step would be?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That's a good question. I would show support for her and empathize with her and her situation. Do you have lunch with her? Can you send her a text letting her know she's valued? She might appreciate that.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Sometimes we have lunch, but usually she just eats at her desk. I could easily send her texts of encouragement.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Ok. That's a good first step. I think you have a way forward with this. Maybe after a few texts, you can sit down and talk about solutions in detail, like going to HR.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Ok. I'll try sending a couple of texts every day. Then I'll see if she wants to have lunch outside with me when the weather gets better.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That's a good positive step. I think that you've got a pretty handle on the situation. Good for you!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thanks. The boss is pretty good at his job. He know how to manage projects and he's good with clients. There is just this one thing that he gets wrong and somebody needs to get him to stop.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I understand that you're frustrated and sad. I would feel the same way too. I think that anyone else in your situation would feel the same way.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thanks. I guess I just have to accept things as they are. I can't afford to leave this job, and it's impossible to find another job at this time. Plus I don't want to abandon my co-worker in her time of need.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Well, you have to do what is right for yourself. If you find a better job, think seriously about taking it. Your co-worker will not be there forever. You have a responsibility to yourself.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: True. I hadn't thought of it that way. I should consider my needs. Thanks!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You're welcome! I'm glad that I was able to help you!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I feel a lot better now. I start sending text messages to my co-worker on Monday. Thanks! Have a good day!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You too! Have a great day, and good luck with work!

situation: I cheated on my boyfriend and I don't want to tell him. I feel really bad because he is a great person.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I am having a really hard time with something that I did to my boyfriend.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Do you want to talk about it?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yes, thank you! I was with him since childhood and we are actually still together. I slipped up one night and hate to admit what I did.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: It sounds like you're holding onto a lot of guilt about this incident.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Are you comfortable talking about that night?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yes, it is intense guilt. I can't think of anything else. I hate to tell you but I cheated on him. I am so sorry and can't look at myself in the mirror.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That can really weigh you down, beating yourself up with guilt.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Does he know about the incident?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I feel like I am worthless and I don't deserve to ever be happy or laugh again. I really do love him and I don't ever want him to find out what I did. Yes, I cant stop beating myself up. He doesn't know what I did but he is little suspicious.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You definitely deserve to laugh and be happy. One mistake doesn't define you!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thank you! A long time ago his sister told me that he cheated on me and I believed her and so I wanted to get him back.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: It says a lot about your character that you feel badly about this, that you can see and recognize you acted in a way you don't agree with. That's something to build on.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That desire to "get even" is definitely understandable.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I appreciate that and have been thinking about character a lot lately. I do believe he cheated on me first, if that even matters now.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Do you feel like you got what you needed from the experience, as in, that you've "gotten even"?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yes, I feel very even! I won't do it again. Can I ever give up the guilt that I feel and move on in our relationship or do I have to tell him what I did? I have known couples who don't tell each other and they are happy now.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: While it may be true in general that honesty is the best policy, in this case I wouldn't suggest telling him. It is just going to cause angry feelings.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: But, the fact you already believed he cheated on you and it motivated you to do the same shows a basic lack of trust in this relationship. That's something you should really look at.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thank you for making me feel heard. I have been alone in my thoughts and needed to be understood. Yes, I agree with you and I feel like I kind of let it go now that I told you about it.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I am going to be in touch regularly of that's okay? I feel so much better now. I can take a deep breath again and I want to go take a walk with my dog now. Thank you for all of your time and understanding.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I'm glad you're feeling better now that you've gotten it out in the open. Of course, you are welcome anytime.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That sounds great because I want to keep this private and I know you won't tell anyone so I feel really safe here.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Absolutely. This is all in complete confidence.

situation: turned in ex gf to cps for giving her kids drugs.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Hello! Hope you are doing well. How may I assist you?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: my recent ex gf gave her daughters drugs while on a video chat with me. While being very dishonest in our relationship, i am devestated about the truth of all of it now that its over. I really loved her. and her kids. we had some great times. I turned her in for giving her kids drugs as thats not okay.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: today im super sad. i dont have any motivation. I dont want to really be around, and im trapped in my thoughts with everything. i had to move to get away from her stalking me. now im in a new place trying to find a job and get set up here. Its difficult to start over.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: its a pretty intense situation i admit.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You are girlfriend is giving drugs to hew own kids. Did I get it right?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That is what she did. Among many other things.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That is really intense, unacceptable. I am feeling sorry for the poor little girl and you.  It looks like there is something wrong with her. I can understand what you are going through.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Its a difficult time. I want to do nothing. It feels very bleek right now worrying about finances and getting set up in a new state.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: It must be hard time for you. You should not worry about her. You should think about your job and the poor girl who is trapped with her.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: her daughters now both live with their dads. so thats taken care of, he kids are safer and better off. I however am devastated that the whole relationship wound up a lie and the whole thing has me really in a bad place mentally.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I felt the same way when I broke up with my friend for some pathetic things which she did. The good part is girls are safe now. Give some time to yourself. Time will heal everything.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thats a hard light to see at the end of the tunnel when things are bleek.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: im not sure what else to do.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: My mind runs away a bit right now, its just all stress ful

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: So sorry to hear about that. You should indulge yourself in some activities like yoga or meditation.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: talk to your family and friends and go to them for a while. It will help you and you can come out of it.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Finding a better job.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: might be a more productive task.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You will get one soon. Keep working on. My best wishes are there with you. Time never stay the same.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: you dont want to continue to talk

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: No, that is not the case. You can talk as much as you want.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I see. i thought you had left.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: No I am hear and thinking of you and trying to figure out how can you come out of it.

situation: Girlfriend dumped me for someone else!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Hi, how are you?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Hi there! I'm great, what about you? How can I help today?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: My girlfriend dumped me for someone else! I've been really shook up.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I'm so sorry to hear that :( Do you have any idea why?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content:
I think she just lost interest in me? 
It happened a couple of months ago. I wish I could get over it. I can't get her off my mind. She's dating this total loser.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That's tough... so you're saying that you grew apart a little? Or do you think it was more one-sided?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content:
It probably was more me than her. 
Maybe I should have done more to show her that I cared for her. Am I not romantic enough? Some guys just aren't that way.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I completely understand, and I don't think you should beat yourself up over it!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's a fair point. I shouldn't be too hard on myself.  I didn't really grow up in a romantic family. I don't know how to do that stuff, but maybe I could learn.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Exactly, and remember everyone is different, perhaps you just weren't her right one, and that can go both ways.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Do you want to improve for her? or for yourself do you think?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's true. I really got hung up on her. I thought she was perfect and that we were perfect together. She didn't see it that way. I can't really change who I am, and she couldn't change who she is.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I don't know if I can change myself for her. Maybe there is something basic that she wants that I just don't have? I should probably change for future relationships.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That's a great line of thought, its good that you're able to identify that yourself

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: What do you think I could do to improve myself?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I suggest that you stay exactly the way that you are. I feel like you'd be happier that way and also more likely to find someone who shares the same values as you

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: If you change who you are, it sort of means that your ex still has a hold on you, you know?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Will the same problems show up in the next relationship though? It could be a long search before I find someone that matches with me?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's true. But I feel like I should take away something from this experience.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I guess that depends on you, would you rather compromise for a relationship or find one who'll really last and not have to change?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I think all relationships require compromise and change. No two people are perfectly compatible.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I worry that I have blind spots that I should fix. My ex-GF didn't really specify what the problem was.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That's true, building on that, you could also say that what your ex found to be the problem is not the be-all end-all. What your ex disliked could be what 10000 other girls love in a guy

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's true. I might end up trying to solve a problem that really isn't there. It's not a bug, it's a feature! :-)

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Wow, you put that really nicely!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thanks! You’ve been really helpful today. I can’t thank you enough for your encouragement and insight.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: It was no problem! Thank you for sharing and talking it out!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I feel a lot better now. Have a great day! Bye!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Have a great day :) bye!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: bye!

situation: I am very depressed and despondent because my boyfriend broke up with me and it was right before the wedding. I am close to his family but they won't speak to me know. I am lost without him.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I am very sad and depressed because of a recent breakup. How can I ever forget it and move on.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Oh no. I'm sorry to hear that. What happened?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I thought everything was great and I already had my wedding dress and we planned a wedding and out of nowhere he dumped me. Now his family won't talk to me either.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: that must be horrible. You were already engaged?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yes, we were only engaged for about a month but we have been friends since childhood.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: that sounds really hard. that's a long time to know someone.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: how long ago did you breka up?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yeah, it was basically my whole life. We broke up two nights ago and he already changed his number and blocked my on social media.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: that's going to be tough for the next little bit, those sound like some pretty fresh wounds. Have you been able to talk to friends?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I would like to talk to talk to friends but they happen to be his sisters. So, I am cut off from all of my support. Thank You for talking to me.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I had a break up last year and while it was painful for a bit, i used it as an opportunity to focus on myself and the things I needed. It ended up being a really good self reflective opportunity. Maybe join a gym or a group of some kind to change your network a bit?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I have been wanting to join the gym. Is it hard going alone or do you go with friends? I hate doing things all alone.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I like going to the gym alone but that's just my personal preference. There are fitness groups like bootcamps that you could join and meet new people. Who knows, you may meet a new friend.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That is a good idea. I need to be around other people who have goals like weight loss. My boyfriend never wanted me to join groups and only wanted me to be friends with his family. That is why I am so alone right now. I made bad choices.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That sounds hard. You deserve to have friends of your own.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I agree with you. Do you think that I should try calling his friends and asking for his new number?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: there are lots of different kinds of groups you can join, not just fitness. and you can choose how much you want to actively engage with them. I would suggest taking time for yourself. Maybe give yourself some distance from him. If you both decide to reach out again in the future that may be an opportunity, but definitely take care of you.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yeah, I can see your point of view. I have been obsessed with our relationship. We had a fight and it was a verbal war between us and he wouldn't forgive me afterwards.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: sometimes you just need some distance. I hate that old saying but as it goes "there are plenty of fish in the sea".

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: It is true and I am a really nice person and have turned down other guys for him. I am going to stop thinking about him and just join the gym today. You have opened my eyes!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Good luck! you got this. Have fun with it, it sounds like you have other guys who are after you ;)

situation: My neighbors are all mad at me, because I was the cause of a mutual friend being evicted from our complex.  Her dog attacked my dog and another dog within a week.  I reported the incident to apartment management and Animal Control.  She was told to get rid of her dog or move.  She elected to move, but continued to say that she "got kicked out because of me".  I guess friends liked her more, because now I'm the "bad guy".

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Hello! How are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: It's a pretty day, but I'm sad.  How are you?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I'm doing well, thank you for asking! Do you mind me asking what's going on that's making you feel sad?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I don't like being blamed for something when I don't feel I did anything wrong.  A neighbor's dog attacked my dog and another dog within a week and I reported it to our apartment manager and animal control.  She was given the choice to get rid of her dog or move and she decided to move.  But my neighbors are mad at me, because they think I overreacted.  I guess they liked her more.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: It sounds like you feel as though you are being personally attacked/mistreated for something that you feel you handled correctly. Does that summarize what you're feeling?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Absolutely!  You nailed it!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I feel as though you absolutely did the right thing! If I were in your shoes, I would have done the exact same thing.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thank you!  That makes me feel better!  But, I don't know how to make my neighbors feel that way and I hate being the outcast.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I have neighbors who act in similar ways. They act as though they run the entire street, but they rent just like everyone else does here. Dealing with them can be very difficult at times.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Sounds like we live in the same neighborhood!  Ha!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Just based on what you're saying, I can completely understand why you feel the way that you do. In my opinion, you have every right to feel that way.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Haha! It's not fun at all to have neighbors who make you feel as though you're somehow less than them. I know exactly what that feels like.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yep!  That's how I feel.  What should I do to get back in good graces with them?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I do know people who have gone through the exact same thing in the past. When another animal attacks one of your fur-babies, it's absolutely terrible. However, the fact that this woman was given the option to either get rid of her dog or move tells me that you are 100% correct in the way that you handled this situation. It's unfortunate for the woman, yes, but she should have had more control over her dog. It's bad enough when one animal is attacked, but when it's more than one, that's when you really know that it's a problem. Again, legally, she had to make a choice, and that's because she failed to properly secure her dog. I really don't feel as though you did anything wrong.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: If I were you, I would ask to speak with the neighbors who have an issue with you face-to-face. I know it may be difficult, but I think that would be the best way to go about it. Are you on good terms with the other person whose dog was attacked?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I totally agree with you!  The woman is disabled and I think most of the neighbors feel sorry for her. dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I can feel your worries when you speak about your anxiety, and I am hoping we can find a way to approach your feelings. Do you think there was a specific instance that is causing doubts within yourself or is it in your nature to self-doubt?

dialog.content: It is in my nature to self doubt. Additionally, I have had a few bumps in the road during my program and struggle to pick myself up after those bumps. For example, I am on academic probation and have a certain expectation over the next few months that I have to hit to stay in the program due to struggles in previous classes. I am reaching the bar just fine, according to those that I work with, and often feel I am doing quite well but occasionally I start to panic and think that I am not doing as well as I think that I am and start to spiral

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: This has also been a past struggle of mine, and I think it partially stems from the bar we set so high for ourselves. We obviously cannot reach the top right away, but have to work our way up. Often times, when facing an obstacle, such as a bad grade, we might retreat further down the path due to fear that it will keep happening. I know that I personally had a tendency to think of my family and friends who I feel are dependent on me or comparing myself with others when the only person we should think of in these instances is ourself. When making progress, tiny steps upward matter more than you think. But when we take a step backward, we cannot fall back down to the starting point by letting our doubts pile upon one another.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That is a really good point and helpful. Thank you!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Of course, I am happy to help! I have noticed that letting yourself celebrate a good grade can be really helpful to feel that you are enough and making good progress. But when things do go south, because life certainly happens, we must stay in the present and live life like we usually would, with a drive that we are going to push harder against whatever we have to face next.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That is exactly right. I need to focus more on what progress has been made and not on what progress hasn't been made yet because there is still time to make that progress.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Exactly! I also suggest if you are having doubts, talk it out with someone like your partner or a close friend. They can often provide a pick me up you didn't even know you needed. More than anything, give yourself credit and relax. Take some time to just breathe and release the tension. I guarantee it will leave you feeling refreshed and fulfilled for the next tasks you have to face. I believe you can do this and I feel that you believe it too, which is amazing. As often as it is said, believing in yourself is truly the best foundation to achieve your goals.

situation: My boyfriend just told me that he wants to break up because he doesn't see a future because he never wants to get married but I do.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: How 'd you get over it?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I tried to focus on other areas of my life and to critically reflect on my relationship. Largely, I threw myself into my work and tried to better that aspect of my life, and hung out with people that I knew cared about me. dialog.content: I think one day off to get the crying out of your system is a good emotional cleanse. We all need time to self care and look after our emotions. Maybe going for a walk or doing some yoga, to place your focus on something rather than being boxed into a room may help to shift your focus.

Getting a puppy       In the mean time,  I think it helps to try to keep busy.   Is there goals you can set for yourself each day? situation: I've been so blessed to have such a great job but thanks to Corona, my works has come to a screeching halt.  I'm so anxious because I don't know when my next check will be or for how much.  The pressure and stress is almost too much.

dialog.content: Thank you.  I appreciate your support and words.  That's very true.  Things haven't worked out yet so I just need to hang on.

dialog.speaker: assistant

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: There's so much information and disinformation out there, it can be really hard to discern help.  There's one website: sba.gov that has several different options for pandemic relief for small businesses.  I don't know what are you're in but there are some local resources available from my local government websites too, you might want to check that out too!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That is wonderful!  Thank you so much for passing along that information.  I've written the website down and I'll do some searching tonight!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Just talking to you has brought my anxiety level down so much!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Excellent, I hope you're able to find some relief through this difficult time!  I have a friend who looked into it and was able to find a small grant to help him out!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content:
That's hopeful to hear!  Thank you!  Do you know if there's another stimulus check coming?  Those have helped me so much.  It's also amazing what a kind person, who really listens and wants to help,
 can do for your well being as well!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I have heard that another one is coming, and this one should be better than the last one.  It's all dependent on income, so anyone that makes less than 80k a year should be getting the full amount according to what I've heard.  I agree, they did help!  Would that ease your burden a little bit if the next one comes soon?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Great!  I will definitely qualify if that's the case.  I would be able to pay rent if we get this check soon.  It's been so hard making ends meet.  Sometimes I get so overwhelmed I feel like I can't breathe.  Any tips for that?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Hang in there, I'm sure help is on the way and your positive attitude will help carry you until then, you seem like a really strong person.  For me, I try taking really deep breaths and even meditating if it gets really bad.  Have you ever tried anything like that?

dialog.content: That's so kind of you to say.  I try to stay positive and be strong but I often feel like I'm failing.  It helps to hear words of support that I'm going to make it through this tough time.  I've never meditate before.  Do you listen to music or just silence.  I would love to give that a shot.  I try to take deep breaths.  Oftentimes it helps but only for a moment.  Maybe mediation could help me overall for longer periods of time.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You're not failing as long as you're still trying and not giving up.  All we can do sometimes is pick ourselves back up and keep plugging along.  There are a few apps available that have calming music, stories to listen to,  or even breath exercises to try.  They are all over the app stores.  It definitely helps me!  Meditation has been proven to help a lot of people in stressful situations.

dialog.content: That's so true!  I'm going to write that down and post it on my fridge!  Those are the words I'm going to live by for the next while.  I'll look up some apps!  Thank you for being such a great listener and supporter.  I feel so much lighter and hopeful.  I can't believe how much this has helped me.  I look forward to chatting again soon.  Thank you so much!  You have no idea how grateful I am to you right now.  I won't give up!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Of course, so happy to be of help!  Hopefully we can talk again soon and catch up on how you've been doing.  It helps me a lot to know I'm helping others too, any kind of service always feels nice.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Thank you, you too!

situation: Work is extremely stressful. I work 12 hour days and I feel like I have no time to do the things I like.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Oh wow! Well first of all, I want to say that I definitely empathize with you. My first job out of college was one of the most stressful times of my life. It does get better though.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: What is stressful about your job?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Everyone I've reached out to has said the same. There are several factors that make my job stressful. The biggest issues are working from home and unrealistic deadlines.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Oh wow, that sounds really hard, and I can only imagine it is so difficult to be breaking into a new career entirely working from home. Even people I know who have been in their jobs a long time are struggling with remote work. It is hard if your company is not being flexible on deadlines during this time.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Have you found any strategies that have helped a tiny bit?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content:
It has been very hard in general, training has been a nightmare! I've tried to develop some coping strategies but they seem to fly out the window when i'm really stressed. I tend to
 over eat in these situations aswell.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I totally feel that! I think that has been a struggle for me during quarantine too - the "quarantine fifteen!" I have had to look for other forms of comfort to keep that at bay ... but I did bake cookies tonight :)

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I know I'm just an mturk rando, but the one thing I know that has helped almost everyone I know with career stress is having a mentor. Especially someone who is not at your company. Do you know anyone, even a friend of a friend, who could maybe be a sounding board for your career?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Haha you're a good rando. I would love to have someone to be a guide in my career but most of my family are blue collar workers. Whenever I vent they think its a joke because my job is cushy and pays extremely well. I'm honestly the snowflake in the family.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Oh say no more! I totally get that just based on my own background as well. The only thing I could recommend is to cast a wide net when thinking about mentorship... maybe even a former professor in your field could recommend a former student who graduated a few years before you. Just a thought, but I know for myself, it's nice to have validation if the people I am working with are having unfair expectations.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I hope that helped a bit, and if not, please let me know. We can keep chatting and dig into this a bit more if you'd like. What do you think?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: You definitely given me some food for thought, I didn't think this would go so well lol

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Makes me feel a little less lonely today :)

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I really enjoyed talking with you! Also, I just have to say, we are all having such a hard time with this pandemic. It's not just you - you aren't broken. You are doing your best as we alll are :)

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: omg don't make me cry, I'm tearing up rn. I just want to thank you for the tips!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You are totally welcome! I've been where you are (minus the pandemic). You will make it, and you really will adjust to the stress and also find better jobs over time.

dialog.speaker: user

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Have a goodnight/day where ever you are!

dialog.speaker: assistant

dialog.speaker: user

dialog.content: Sometimes having depression can make a person feel that they need to seclude themselves. Were you aware that he felt this way?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Maybe you could try to talk to him about seeking help for depression? Talking to someone close to him or even a professional would help him immensely. Talking to a counselor would help him learn ways to cope with his feelings.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I understand completely. Depression runs in my family and if it goes untreated, can scare anyone that is involved. You can try suggesting that he talks to a close friend, family member, or counselor to talk about how he is feeling and ways to cope with it. Sometimes medication could be a good way to fix the chemical imbalance that can cause depression.

dialog.content: I will try to help him, he just doesn't want to seem to talk to my anymore. Do you have any suggestions on how to approach it with him?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You could start a conversation by saying that you just want to check on him and see if he is okay and how he is doing. You don't want to start the conversation by immediately telling him to get help, as he might just get defensive.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That is a good approach. And you don't think I will further upset him?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I don't believe it will upset him if you ease into suggesting that he should talk to someone he is close to or seeing a professional. Your main goal is to check on him and make sure that he is okay since he seems to want to be alone and not talk to anyone. Once he tells you how he is doing you could mention that even talking to someone about his thoughts and feelings could lift a weight off his shoulders. If he seems that he doesn't really know why he feels the way does, you could suggest that medically speaking, sometimes our brains don't produce the chemicals that we need them to and it could make us feel bad. Seeing a professional to get their opinion would also help greatly.

situation: My husband quit his job to stay home with our teenage son who is very difficult and has been in legal trouble.  My husband feels like it is important for him to be home more but this is putting a financial strain on us and our son is causing a lot of emotion conflict.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Hello, I am looking for help in managing some depression.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You've come to a good place to start.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Is there something in particular that is leaving you feeling depressed?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I am feeling resentful towards my husband for quitting his job, and my son for being the reason that he quit his job.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: My son's behavior is causing a lot of conflict in our house and my husband not working is putting a lot of financial stress on us

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I'm sorry to hear that. Feeling resentment for both your husband's loss of work and your son's loss of his job is entirely understandable.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I have a hard time not dwelling on the negative things going on.  It's hard to see anything positive or feel hopeful for the future.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I have an adult son who also lost his job recently, and I understand the added stress that can cause in your household.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That is a really difficult position to be in.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: My son is very defiant and argues with us about everything we try and get him to do, but he expects us to give him money for fun things that he wants to do when he isn't willing to work or even help out around the house

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Regardless of what your husband's actions are and your son's, could you possibly make the time to do things to support yourself, that even momentarily would provide you with some positive feelings?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I try to go for a run every night, that does help.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That is very hard emotionally, so I am sorry you have to deal with your son's behavior.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: It is hard to keep my emotions separate from what is going on with him

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That is very understandable! Setting boundaries for yourself may help. Even when it's difficult, being clear with your son that you will not be giving him money could help.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Sometimes being clear with your own expectations can help him to see what won't be accepted from him as long as he is living in your home.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yes, I could do better at that.  I want him to have fun and do things with friends, but I want him to be willing to work for what he wants, too.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I do struggle with expectations.  I want him to be someone that he is not willing to be, I need to let go of that and just let him be himself and not base my emotions on his choices.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I completely understand that. Especially when it comes to people we are close to, it can be hard to separate our expectations from theirs.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I feel guilty sometimes taking time to do things that I like and that are away from my family.  I can see that I need to do that more, though.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I've had to learn this lesson many times with my own children. It's difficult, but ultimately will give you some relief from your resentment.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thank you, it is helpful to know that other people have felt the same way.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Absolutely! Being a parent is difficult. Remembering to support yourself helps. Making the time to do things to support your wellbeing doesn't make you selfish at all

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thank you!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You are certainly not alone.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Has any of this been helpful? Is there more I can do for you?
